data.name: rating rules oracle — headless (the CI-safe pipeline stage)
data.description:
Run the `rating` rulebook over each saved scenario and assert the monthly premium matches an INDEPENDENT
expectation (so a calc.js drift turns a row red — a real oracle test, not calc-vs-itself). Every row's
`Rule.execute` emits the rulebook's `calc.req` hits, so the run's RTM lights each requirement the saved
scenarios reach — RATE-001/1 (young-driver loading) and RATE-002/1 (prior-claims surcharge) — while the
senior-driver arm (RATE-001/2) stays the honest gap no scenario exercises. The REST covering-array
coverage (checks/quotes.feature) and this requirement coverage ride the SAME run report, so the published
report carries the Coverage view AND the Traceability RTM together.

data.name: <id> — <label>
young-collision-ca — young driver, collision, California — the young-driver loading fires
young-collision-ca — young driver, collision, California — the young-driver loading fires
prior-claims-comprehensive-ny — adult with prior claims, comprehensive, New York — the prior-claims surcharge fires
prior-claims-comprehensive-ny — adult with prior claims, comprehensive, New York — the prior-claims surcharge fires
clean-liability-tx — adult, clean record, liability, Texas — the base case, no loadings
clean-liability-tx — adult, clean record, liability, Texas — the base case, no loadings

data.name: auto quotes — the covering-array dimension demo (REST front door to the rating engine)
data.description:
# Six quotes across coverage × state × priorClaims. MARGINALLY this looks fully exercised — every
# coverage, every state, both prior-claims values appear at least once (each axis 100%). Yet only 6 of
# the 3×5×2 = 30 input COMBINATIONS are tested. The cross/point view surfaces the gap; the covering-array
# deck answers it with the MINIMAL pairwise set of cells to test next. This is the Clip 1 screenshot.

data.name: quote a <coverage> policy in <state> (priorClaims=<priorClaims>)
quote a COLLISION policy in CA (priorClaims=false)
quote a COLLISION policy in CA (priorClaims=false)
quote a COMPREHENSIVE policy in NY (priorClaims=true)
quote a COMPREHENSIVE policy in NY (priorClaims=true)
quote a LIABILITY policy in TX (priorClaims=false)
quote a LIABILITY policy in TX (priorClaims=false)
quote a COLLISION policy in FL (priorClaims=true)
quote a COLLISION policy in FL (priorClaims=true)
quote a COMPREHENSIVE policy in WA (priorClaims=false)
quote a COMPREHENSIVE policy in WA (priorClaims=false)
quote a LIABILITY policy in CA (priorClaims=true)
quote a LIABILITY policy in CA (priorClaims=true)
auto quotes — the covering-array dimension demo (REST front door to the rating engine)

request:
1 > POST http://localhost:44441/quotes
1 > Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
1 > Host: localhost:44441
1 > Content-Length: 72
1 > Connection: keep-alive
1 > User-Agent: Apache-HttpClient/5.6.1 (Java/21.0.11)
{
  "state": "CA",
  "coverage": "COLLISION",
  "driverAge": 40,
  "priorClaims": false
}

response time in milliseconds: 104
1 < 201 POST http://localhost:44441/quotes
1 < content-length: 82
1 < Content-Type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
{
  "id": "#(uuid())",
  "policyClass": "PREFERRED",
  "monthlyPremium": 100,
  "currency": "USD"
}

data.name: insurance Policy API — quote → bind → claim lifecycle (REST / OpenAPI)
data.description:
# baseUrl is the in-process Policy API mock (karate-config.js auto-starts mock/policy-mock.feature).
# Each scenario exercises an OpenAPI operation so cov.openapi grades which were hit. cancelPolicy
# (DELETE /policies/{id}) and getClaim (GET /claims/{id}) are left as the worklist Coverage.gaps() shows.
